interface.name: aether_shell_manager
interface.description: This global interface is responsible for managing shell-specific features that dictate window arrangements, such as setting reserved areas (struts) around the screen edges and changing layouts.

request.name: set_reserved_areas

description.summary: set forbidden edge margins on an output

request.description:
Request that the compositor reserve pixel bands along the edges of the output identified by output_id. No window — tiled, floating, or maximised — will be placed inside the reserved bands.
All values are in logical pixels. Pass 0 for any edge to clear that margin. The compositor replies with a reserved_areas_applied event once it has recalculated the work area and re-arranged all windows.

arg.name: top
arg.summary: reserved pixels from the top edge

arg.name: bottom
arg.summary: reserved pixels from the bottom edge

arg.name: left
arg.summary: reserved pixels from the left edge

arg.name: right
arg.summary: reserved pixels from the right edge

event.name: reserved_areas_applied
event.description: Sent in response to set_reserved_areas after the compositor has updated the work area for the given output and re-arranged windows. Echo of the values that were actually applied.

arg.name: top
arg.summary: applied top margin in logical pixels

arg.name: bottom
arg.summary: applied bottom margin in logical pixels

arg.name: left
arg.summary: applied left margin in logical pixels

arg.name: right
arg.summary: applied right margin in logical pixels

request.name: set_layout

description.summary: set the active window layout on an output

request.description:
Request that the compositor switch the active layout on the output identified by output_id.
layout_index is a zero-based index into the compositor's internal layouts[] array. The compositor validates the index; out-of-range values are silently ignored. A layout_state event is broadcast to all bound clients after a successful change.

arg.name: layout_index
arg.summary: zero-based index into the layouts array

event.name: layout_state
event.description:
Sent once immediately after the client binds the interface, and again whenever the active layout on any output changes.
layout_symbols is a newline-separated list of all available layout symbols in index order (e.g. "[]=\n><>\n[M]"). layout_index is the zero-based index of the currently active layout.
